sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः। क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत॥
transliteration: yathā prakāśayaty ekaḥ kṛtsnaṃ lokam imaṃ raviḥ | kṣetraṃ kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṃ prakāśayati bhārata ||
literal_meaning: As the one sun illuminates this entire world, so the Knower-of-the-field (soul) illuminates the whole field (body), O Bhārata.
contextual_meaning: Consciousness is not generated by the body; it is the invisible ‘sun’ of the self that suffuses every cell with aliveness. When this inner sun rises, the body-world is seen; when it sets, only darkness remains.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic clarity—after confronting mortality, Arjuna now senses the imperishable light that outlives the battlefield body.
trigger_scenario: Waking up from general anesthesia and realizing, ‘I’m back!’—the instant re-kindling of inner light that makes the whole body feel mine again.
anchor_text: Your inner light, not the body, makes you alive.
